LIBERTYVILLE MANOR EXT CARE

CCN: 145344 · LIBERTYVILLE, IL 60048 · Lake County

OverviewRatingsInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
610 PETERSON ROAD, LIBERTYVILLE, IL 60048
Phone
8473676100
Certified beds
174
Avg daily residents
27 (16% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1978-04-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-21 10 10 0 197 2 296
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-08 12 7 5 211 2 317
Total weighted health score
301.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (27)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

23 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-02-18 2025-03-21
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-01-21 2025-03-21
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. K 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-01-21 2025-04-25
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2025-01-21 2025-04-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-01-21 2025-02-28
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-12-09 2024-12-31
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-04-17 2024-06-03
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2024-02-08 2024-03-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-02-08 2024-05-10
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. G 2024-02-08 2024-03-31
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-02-08 2024-03-31
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2024-02-08 2024-04-11
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. F 2024-02-08 2024-03-31
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2024-02-08 2024-05-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2023-10-20 2023-10-21
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2023-10-20 2023-11-30
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-03-22 2023-04-28
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-03-22 2023-04-28
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-03-22 2023-04-28
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2023-03-22 2023-04-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-03-22 2023-04-28

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
